---
label: "Vibe Coding Foundations"
icon: "🎸"
---

## Beyond "Ask and Pray": How Vibe Coding Actually Works

Vibe coding isn't typing a prompt and hoping for the best. It's a **collaboration methodology** — treating Claude Code as a junior developer who types at 200 WPM, has read every library's documentation, but needs clear direction, active review, and architectural guardrails.

<AnalogyBox emoji="🏗️" title="Construction Foreman, Not Magic Wand">
You're the construction foreman. Claude Code is the crew — incredibly fast, never tired, knows every building technique. But without blueprints, specifications, and your active oversight, they'll build a structurally unsound house very quickly. The foreman's job isn't to lay bricks — it's to ensure every brick goes in the right place.
</AnalogyBox>

### The Mental Model Shift

<ComparisonTable title="Beginner vs Master Vibe Coder" columns={['Aspect','Beginner','Master']} rows={[
  ['Prompt style','Vague: "Build me a login page"','Specific: "Build a login page using NextAuth.js with Google OAuth, session stored in Postgres via Prisma, redirect to /dashboard on success"'],
  ['Review approach','Accept everything blindly','Review every plan before execution, course-correct actively'],
  ['Error handling','Debug the AI output manually','Ask Claude to explain its approach first, catch issues in planning'],
  ['Architecture','Let Claude decide structure','Define architecture upfront in CLAUDE.md, Claude follows your patterns'],
  ['Iteration','One giant prompt','Break into small, verifiable steps'],
]}/>

### The CLAUDE.md File: Your Project's Memory

The single most important file in any vibe coding project. CLAUDE.md is Claude Code's persistent memory — it reads this file at the start of every session to understand your project's rules, patterns, and constraints.

<CodeBlock language="markdown" label="Example CLAUDE.md for a Next.js Project" b64="" />

<AnalogyBox emoji="📋" title="CLAUDE.md = Your Data Dictionary">
In MDM, the data dictionary defines field rules, valid values, and business constraints. CLAUDE.md serves the same purpose for your codebase — it tells Claude what patterns to follow, what files to leave alone, and what constraints are non-negotiable. Without it, Claude invents its own patterns every session.
</AnalogyBox>

<Quiz
  question="You start a new Claude Code session without a CLAUDE.md file. What's the most likely outcome?"
  options={["Claude will remember your project from last time", "Claude will invent its own patterns and conventions, potentially conflicting with your existing code", "Claude will refuse to work without a CLAUDE.md", "Nothing changes — CLAUDE.md is optional and doesn't affect output"]}
  correctIndex={1}
  explanation="Without CLAUDE.md, Claude has no persistent memory of your project's conventions. It will make reasonable but potentially inconsistent choices — different naming conventions, different file organization, different error handling patterns. Over multiple sessions, this creates spaghetti code. CLAUDE.md is technically optional but practically essential."
/>

<SeeItInRe3 text="Re³ has its own CLAUDE.md and ARCHITECTURE.md that define the debate pipeline, agent roles, and file structure. Every Claude Code session on Re³ starts by reading these files." />
